title: Characterization of ABC-minimal trees
statement_text: Characterize all trees with minimum ABC-index (ABC-minimal trees), i.e., for each order $n$, describe every tree $T$ of order $n$ that minimizes $\mathrm{ABC}(T) = \sum_{uv \in E(T)} \sqrt{\frac{d_u + d_v - 2}{d_u d_v}}$.
context_text: Despite many attempts over several years, characterizing trees with minimum ABC-index remained an open problem in mathematical chemistry, described by some as one of the most intriguing open problems in the area. A computer-aided study (reference [24]) produced a structural conjecture that was subsequently disproved by later work [2, 3], indicating the true structure is more complex.
paper_contribution: Fully resolves the problem by describing the exact structure of ABC-minimal trees for all sufficiently large $n$: radius at most 5, all vertices except one of degree at most 54, all but $O(1)$ vertices of degree 1, 2, 4, or 53, and $\gamma_n = \frac{1}{53}\sqrt{\frac{1+26\sqrt{\frac{55+156}{106}}}{365}}\,n + O(1) \approx 0.67737178\,n + O(1)$.
notes: PDF source — no formal problem-environment label; described in introductory prose as an open problem. Math in the asymptotic formula may be garbled due to PDF extraction.
paper_title: The evolution of the structure of ABC-minimal trees

title: B3-branches main component conjecture
statement_text: $B_3$-branches are the main structural component of ABC-minimal trees.
context_text: Earlier computational and theoretical works (references [24] and [13]) expected ABC-minimal trees to be composed primarily of $B_3$-branches, with only a small number of $B_k$-branches for $k \neq 3$. Reference [1] later indicated that $C$-branches would play a prominent role, but the full picture was not established.
paper_contribution: Disproves the conjecture for sufficiently large $n$ by proving (Theorem 5.3) that for large trees only $C_{52}$-branches occur as the dominant structural component, so $B_3$-branches are not the main building block in the asymptotic regime.
notes: PDF source — statement reconstructed from introductory prose; bibliography not available in extraction so authors of [24] and [13] cannot be identified precisely.
paper_title: The evolution of the structure of ABC-minimal trees
